steps.title: Translate no real solutions into an inequality
steps.content: The question asks for the greatest positive integer $b$ that makes the quadratic have no real solution. For a quadratic, no real solution means the graph does not cross the $x$-axis, so the discriminant must be negative: $b^2-4ac\lt0$. Here $a=-3$ and $c=-27$, so $$b^2-4(-3)(-27)\lt0.$$

steps.title: Find the allowable values of b
steps.content: Simplify the inequality: $$b^2-324\lt0$$ $$b^2\lt324.$$ Thus $-18\lt b\lt18$. Since the question says $b$ is a positive integer, the possible values are $1$ through $17$.

steps.title: Pick the largest integer
steps.content: The largest positive integer less than $18$ is $17$. At $b=18$, the discriminant is $0$, which would give one real solution instead of no real solution.

steps.title: Check the boundary
steps.content: With $b=17$, the discriminant is $17^2-324=-35$, which is negative, so the equation has no real solution. Therefore the greatest possible value is 17.
